meta.name: Preprocess & cluster (raw counts)
meta.description: Turn raw counts into Leiden clusters with a UMAP embedding (normalize → log1p → HVG → PCA → neighbors → Leiden → UMAP). For unprocessed data such as Xenium — not for already-normalized sessions.
meta.provenance: Standard scanpy preprocessing-to-clustering path; targets unprocessed raw-count data such as Xenium.

readme: Scanpy path from raw counts to Leiden clusters plus a UMAP embedding. No explicit HVG step: PCA uses an existing `highly_variable` flag if present (Visium) and all genes otherwise (targeted panels like Xenium have too few genes for a fixed n_top_genes). Leiden clustering runs on the graspologic (MIT) implementation, not scanpy's GPL igraph/leidenalg backends.

params.name: cluster_key
params.tooltip: obs column to write the Leiden cluster labels into

params.name: resolution
params.tooltip: Leiden resolution: higher = more, smaller clusters
